# cloudflare


<!-- WARNING: THIS FILE WAS AUTOGENERATED! DO NOT EDIT! -->

## CF

Thin wrapper around the official `cloudflare` Python SDK. Token is read
from the `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` environment variable by default.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/cfeasy/blob/main/cfeasy/core.py#L13"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### CF

``` python

def CF(
    token:NoneType=None, timeout:int=10, kw:VAR_KEYWORD
):

```

*Cloudflare API wrapper using the official cloudflare Python SDK*

### Least-privilege token setup

Create a **Custom Token** in the Cloudflare dashboard (My Profile → API
Tokens → Create Token):

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 50%" />
<col style="width: 50%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Capability</th>
<th>Required permission</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><code>dns_read</code> / DNS ops</td>
<td>Zone → DNS → <strong>Edit</strong> (scope to specific zone)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>account_read</code> / tunnel ops</td>
<td>Account → Account Settings → <strong>Read</strong></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><code>tunnel_read</code> / tunnel ops</td>
<td>Account → Cloudflare Tunnel → <strong>Edit</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Use `CF().verify()` to confirm your token has the permissions it needs
before running ops.

``` python
c = CF()
c.verify()
```

    {'result': True}

``` python
# setup_tunnel: subdomain
tid, token = c.setup_tunnel('sankalpa.sh', 'app')   # → app.sankalpa.sh
tid, token
```

``` python
# setup_tunnel: apex domain (no name → CNAME-flattened at root)
tid_apex, token_apex = c.setup_tunnel('sankalpa.sh')   # → example.com
tid_apex, token_apex
```

    ('6791fbaf-e848-422b-8fc7-35afc3e435d0',
     'eyJhIjoiOTg3ODZiMGRlMTM5OTE0YmVkNGMyYjdmNGYwYzg4YjkiLCJ0IjoiNjc5MWZiYWYtZTg0OC00MjJiLThmYzctMzVhZmMzZTQzNWQwIiwicyI6IjNNOVhaQm1IWkJPdDl3a28rRUxza3dmaWZ3ZUpqcmpSV0lxdDNRcExwTHFEZVJxaFRyeGRWZnR3M082TzJ1cFNOc0FYSXNRMGx0UjdhaE1SbVFxNDNnPT0ifQ==')

``` python
# tunnel_id: look up by name; delete_tunnel: clean up by name
c.tunnel_id('app')                          # → UUID
c.delete_tunnel(c.tunnel_id('app'))
c.delete_tunnel(c.tunnel_id('sankalpa.sh'))
```

## Helpers

[`repo_root`](https://vedicreader.github.io/cfeasy/core.html#repo_root)
finds the nearest `.git` ancestor.
[`mv_skill_md`](https://vedicreader.github.io/cfeasy/core.html#mv_skill_md)
copies the bundled `SKILL.md` into a project’s agent skill directories
so coding agents pick it up automatically.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/cfeasy/blob/main/cfeasy/core.py#L123"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### mv_skill_md

``` python

def mv_skill_md(
    dry_run:bool=True, dir:NoneType=None
)->None:

```

*Copy bundled SKILL.md to .agents/skills/cfeasy/ and
.claude/skills/cfeasy/ at project root.*

target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

